entity.summary: Centralized cryptocurrency exchange publicly launched in November 2016, associated with Ukrainian founder Vitaliy Bodnar and later operating from a United Kingdom-facing exchange profile. BTC-Alpha suffered a reported hacker attack in November 2021, after which user funds were reported safe and the exchange continued operating; the original btc-alpha.com domain now redirects to ALP.COM.
entity.notes: HEI keeps BTC-Alpha as active because the original domain redirects to ALP.COM and no verified shutdown was recovered. The ALP.COM relationship is treated as current branding or domain continuation rather than a separate canonical successor during this batch.

events.title: BTC-Alpha publicly launched
events.description: BTC-Alpha's public launch is recorded as November 1, 2016 in exchange-profile and secondary reference material.
events.notes: Launch date is taken from the best recovered profile source; no first-party launch notice was recovered in this batch.

events.title: BTC-Alpha reported a hacker attack
events.description: Russian-language crypto reporting described a hacker attack against BTC-Alpha and reported that user funds were safe.
events.notes: The incident is recorded as a security event, not as a terminal event, because the exchange continued operating afterward.

events.title: BTC-Alpha domain redirected to ALP.COM
events.description: The original BTC-Alpha domain redirected to ALP.COM during the 2026 review, indicating current branding or domain continuation.
events.notes: This is a verification marker, not a rebrand finality claim.

evidence.title: BTC-Alpha reference profile
evidence.notes: Reference profile states that BTC-Alpha publicly launched on November 1, 2016.

evidence.title: BTC-Alpha was attacked by hackers and user funds were safe
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous Russian-language reporting on the November 2021 BTC-Alpha attack.

evidence.title: BTC-Alpha exchange review
evidence.notes: Exchange profile preserves the BTC-Alpha identity and United Kingdom exchange profile details.

evidence.title: BTC-Alpha domain redirect to ALP.COM
evidence.notes: During review the original BTC-Alpha domain redirected to ALP.COM.
